item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awww, why'd they deny the wig? I can't imagine it would be any more dangerous than art supplies or plastic jewelry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is my favorite piece of 3165, by a wonderful artist!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Straightforward and creepy. Just wish there was an image to go with it… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The phalanges of its hands are twice the length of an average adult human's, with fingernails three times more durable and possessing narrowed ends for clawing and scratching.

I think you can do without the "for clawing and scratching" bit, but otherwise well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel it works really well in this piece for two reasons - one metaphorical and one narrative.

1) as a metaphor for victims of abusive relationships
2) the creature exhibiting canine tendencies, makes sense with how dogs will show a heavy preference for a specific gender and wariness toward the other based on past abuse/neglect.

I just wish those were maybe explored a bit more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was really leery as soon as I read Canis lupus, thinking "Christ, not another goddamn werewolf"… but your incredibly vivid description and shamelessly effective evoking of sympathy got me. This one will stick with me for a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3165 demonstrates a desire for physical contact with female personnel, and will grasp at them in a wild manner.

No homo?

Physical contact with SCP-3165 is encouraged if a guard is present.

ALL OF THE HOMO!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally don't find it a particularly frequently used trope on this site. In media in general, maybe - but not here. Again, just my opinion, not a fact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice, but how about not making one more female creature that dislikes males? As a woman, I find that cliche to be irritating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see; would that still apply if the upper jaw isn't fused? It's detached from the skull and can move independently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quick note on the description. Only the lower jawbone is referred to as the mandible (and singular). In vertebrates, the upper jaw bone is the maxilla, and isn't secured by musculature but fused with other bones as part of the skull. Now, the way you described it could refer to it being just that weird, but it definitely caught my attention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I always mix those two up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one doesn't really do much for me personally, but it seems like that might just be me. I don't really feel like there's a proper hook, the sympathy it evokes is pretty generic for me, without enough real punch to get through being pretty standard, and I feel like some hints at how she came to be this way would really serve it well.

That said, it's very well written and has a lot of honest strength behind it. The descriptions are evocative, the science babble is generally solid, and the tone is excellent. One small note on the science babble though.

The metacarpals of SCP-3165's feet are extended

Metacarpals are hands. Feet would be metatarsals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
thank you!
also, you're right; I'll edit that little portion!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very nice imagery, your descriptiveness is wonderful. I liked it very much. Its not a brand new idea, but its short and you get a sense of it quickly in terms of appearance and story. You also use technical terms with the drugs and the animals in the right places, love it.

+1

But also, 'panties' isn't clinical and is out of place with the tone, or at least in the way that english is in my area. Perhaps underwear?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fucking horrifying. It's been a while since I've seen a fairly straight-forward monster SCP done this well. I think the dichotomy between the sympathy it evokes and the fact that it's person-eating beast makes this a fun ride.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm having a hard time trying to visualize this creature. Anyone wanna take a stab at drawing it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to see this on the mainsite DogTeeth. Really spooked me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me a bit of the Hunger Doggo tales but a tad bit more unfortunate and, obviously, very much different story.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The new ending works a lot better than the old one - the climax works well, and the reader is left with a feeling that 3165 is more complex than she looks. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad that the concept worked out despite its rocky start when I saw this as a mere draft.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is very intrigueing in the fact that it leaves it somewhere in the middle whether this SCP is:

- A person anomalously made to be more wolf and wishes to be a person again or.
- A wolf attempting to anomalously become more human and is now stuck because it can't get more people to assimilate humanity from…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: made collapsible!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still think you should make it collapsible.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first ever SCP! Special thanks to the Site 19 chat for assisting me in editing and improving this article.
